Publishing to PyPI
==================

You can publish **simpleLOMs** to PyPI in two ways: automatically via GitHub Actions (recommended) or manually from your machine.

Option 1: Publish via GitHub Actions (recommended)
--------------------------------------------------

The repo‚Äôs **Release** workflow builds the package and publishes to PyPI when you run it or when you publish a GitHub release.

**One-time setup**

1. **Create a PyPI account** (if you don‚Äôt have one): https://pypi.org/account/register/

2. **Use Trusted Publishing (no API token needed):**
   - On PyPI: **Account settings ‚Üí Publishing ‚Üí Add a new pending publisher**
   - Choose **GitHub**.
   - **Owner**: ``elizabethkunz``
   - **Repository name**: ``simpleLOMs``
   - **Workflow name**: ``release.yml``
   - **Environment name**: leave empty (or use ``release`` if you create that environment)
   - Click **Add**.
   - In your GitHub repo, go to **Settings ‚Üí Actions ‚Üí General**. Under ‚ÄúWorkflow permissions‚Äù, choose **Read and write permissions**, then save.

3. **Create the project on PyPI (first time only):**
   - Go to https://pypi.org/project/manage/ and **Add new project**.
   - Project name: **simpleLOMs** (must match ``name`` in ``pyproject.toml``).
   - After you add the trusted publisher above, the project will be linked when the workflow runs.

**Publishing**

- **From the Actions tab:** **Actions ‚Üí Release ‚Üí Run workflow** (branch: ``main``) ‚Üí **Run workflow**. This builds the package and publishes to PyPI.
- **From a release:** **Releases ‚Üí Create a new release** (tag e.g. ``v0.1.0``, title optional). When you click **Publish release**, the workflow runs and publishes to PyPI (and deploys docs if GitHub Pages is set up).

Option 2: Publish manually
--------------------------

**One-time**

1. Create a PyPI account: https://pypi.org/account/register/
2. Install build tools: ``pip install build twine``
3. (Optional) Create an API token: PyPI **Account settings ‚Üí API tokens** (use ‚ÄúAdd API token‚Äù, scope: entire account or just this project).

**Each release**

1. **Bump version** in ``pyproject.toml`` (e.g. ``version = "0.1.1"``).

2. **Build the package:**

   .. code-block:: bash

      python -m build

   This creates ``dist/simpleLOMs-0.1.1.tar.gz`` and ``dist/simpleLOMs-0.1.1-py3-none-any.whl``.

3. **Upload to PyPI** (replace ``YOUR_TOKEN`` with your API token or use ``__token__`` and paste when prompted):

   .. code-block:: bash

      twine upload dist/* -u __token__ -p YOUR_TOKEN

   For **Test PyPI** first:

   .. code-block:: bash

      twine upload --repository testpypi dist/* -u __token__ -p YOUR_TEST_PYPI_TOKEN

   Then install with: ``pip install -i https://test.pypi.org/simple/ simpleLOMs``

4. **Commit and tag** (optional but good practice):
   .. code-block:: bash

      git add pyproject.toml
      git commit -m "Bump version to 0.1.1"
      git tag v0.1.1
      git push origin main --tags

After publishing, anyone can install with:

.. code-block:: bash

   pip install simpleLOMs
